query: what was Concorde's maximum speed in miles per hour?
description: Speed is given in km/h and Mach; the answer in mph requires a unit conversion not present on the page. Regex-plan can extract the raw number but cannot derive mph.

direct_anthropic.query: what was Concorde's maximum speed in miles per hour?

snippets: average cruise speed of Mach 2.02 (2,150 km/h; 1,330 mph), more than twice the speed of conventional aircraft
limited the top speed to Mach 2.02

synthesis.summary: According to the Wikipedia article's specifications section, Concorde's maximum speed was 1,354 mph (equivalent to Mach 2.04, or 2,179 km/h / 1,177 knots), described as temperature-limited due to aerodynamic heating constraining the aluminium airframe. Its average operational cruise speed was slightly lower at Mach 2.02 (approximately 1,330–1,338 mph).
items: Maximum speed: 1,354 mph (converted from Mach 2.04 / 2,179 km/h)
synthesis.notes: The article distinguishes between the absolute maximum speed of 1,354 mph (Mach 2.04, temperature-limited) and the typical cruise speed of ~1,330–1,341 mph (Mach 2.02). Both figures are drawn from the aircraft's official performance specifications on Wikipedia.

synthesis.summary: According to Wikipedia's article on Concorde, the aircraft had a maximum cruising speed of Mach 2.02, which is approximately 2,154 km/h or 1,338 mph. An average cruise speed figure of 1,330 mph (Mach 2.02 / 2,150 km/h) is also cited in another passage. These figures represent cruising speed optimised for fuel efficiency rather than an absolute top speed record.
items: ~1,338 mph (2,154 km/h) — cruising speed for optimum fuel consumption
~1,330 mph (2,150 km/h) — average cruise speed cited separately
synthesis.notes: The snippets consistently describe Concorde's 'maximum cruising speed' rather than an absolute top speed. The slight variation between 1,330 mph and 1,338 mph appears to reflect rounding differences across different passages in the same Wikipedia article. Concorde's absolute maximum speed (e.g. during test flights) may have been higher but is not mentioned in these snippets.

direct_byllm.query: what was Concorde's maximum speed in miles per hour?

snippets: Mach 2.02 (~2,154 km/h or 1,338 mph) cruising speed for optimum fuel consumption (supersonic drag minimum and turbojet engines are more efficient at higher speed); fuel consumption at Mach 2 (2,120 km/h; 1,320 mph) and at altitude of 60,000 feet (18,000 m) was 4,800 US gallons per hour (18,000 L/h).
Concorde had a maximum cruising altitude of 18,300 metres (60,000 ft) and an average cruise speed of Mach 2.02 (2,150 km/h; 1,330 mph), more than twice the speed of conventional aircraft.
The normal landing speed was 170 miles per hour (274 km/h).

synthesis.summary: According to the Wikipedia article on Concorde, the aircraft's cruising speed was Mach 2.02, which is approximately 1,330–1,338 mph (2,150–2,154 km/h). This was its operational maximum cruise speed. The article does not separately state a higher absolute maximum speed distinct from the cruise speed, as Mach 2.02 represented both the design cruise and effective top speed in service.
items: Concorde cruise speed: Mach 2.02 (~1,338 mph / 2,154 km/h)
Concorde average cruise speed (flight characteristics section): Mach 2.02 (1,330 mph / 2,150 km/h)
Concorde fuel consumption speed reference: Mach 2 (1,320 mph / 2,120 km/h)
Concorde normal landing speed: 170 mph (274 km/h)
synthesis.notes: The article gives slightly varying mph figures for Mach 2.02 in different sections (1,330 mph and 1,338 mph), likely due to rounding. The top speed cited is the cruising speed; no separate higher maximum speed figure is provided in the extracted HTML content.
